finishing.note: Finishing is half-noise until ~this many shots; the leaderboard is regressed.

rebound.note: A 2019-20 tracking change ~doubled recorded rebounds; the metric is era-neutral (rebounds_prevented_vs_avg).

scoreEffects.note: Trailing teams shoot more at lower quality; score-adjusted xG weights this out.

onIce.note: On-ice / relative xG cover only these seasons; raw on-ice% is team-confounded -- prefer rel_xg_pct.

concurrentValidity.note: Team-summed WAR vs actual regular-season goal differential. High = the value system reproduces real team strength (not just plausible leaders). Same-season decomposition fit, not a forecast -- for the predictive test see walk_forward.

yearOverYearStability.basis: total WAR excluding the fixed career-finishing component
yearOverYearStability.note: Single-season WAR persistence, measured on the re-estimated part of WAR. Moderate by design (single-season RAPM is noisy -- the multi-year RAPM is the stable read). Finishing is excluded here because it is a fixed career constant; including it would inflate persistence (a constant vs itself).

modelCalibration.note: Brier score: lower is better; 0.25 is a coin flip. A 'well_calibrated' verdict means predicted probabilities match observed rates across deciles.

aging_curve_oos.verdict: the aging curve does NOT beat a flat next-season forecast (season variance dwarfs the ~0.1 WAR/yr aging signal) -- its value is the multi-year cumulative shape, not single-year prediction. Read projections as directional.

value_persistence.basis: early vs late average WAR, excluding the fixed career-finishing constant
value_persistence.verdict: WAR persists moderately (r ~ 0.54, finishing excluded) -- the value surplus rests on is real, not noise, but a single season is noisy, so the stable multi-year average is the honest read. Finishing is a fixed career constant; folding it in inflates this to ~0.70 (a constant correlated with itself), so it is reported separately, not as the headline.

honesty_audit.risk: The $/WAR rate is fit to actual cap hits, so 'fair value' partly reflects the same market it judges.
honesty_audit.mitigation: The independent claim is that VALUE (WAR) is externally valid (team WAR vs goal diff r~0.85) and persistent (value_persistence r~0.5). Surplus then asks 'is this player paid like his real production?', not 'does the market agree with itself'.

honesty_audit.risk: The marginal $/WAR moved ~12% ($4.54M -> $5.10M) when one season was added -- the dollar figures are not stable to the decimal.
honesty_audit.mitigation: Self-check refits the rate every run and forces a re-bake on >10% drift; surplus is presented as a range, not a point estimate.

honesty_audit.risk: The aging curve does not improve single-season WAR forecasts out-of-sample (it ties a naive flat baseline).
honesty_audit.mitigation: Documented; the curve is used only for multi-year cumulative projection (where the tendency compounds), labelled directional.

honesty_audit.risk: Players who decline lose ice time or leave, so the aging curve UNDERSTATES real decline (the delta method's known bias).
honesty_audit.mitigation: Stated in the aging-curve scope; projections of veteran out-years are, if anything, optimistic -- not oversold.

honesty_audit.risk: WAR is historical but the cap hit is the current contract, so fresh extensions off limited samples read as overpays.
honesty_audit.mitigation: Flagged per-player; the aging projection partially addresses it by valuing the contract's future, not just the measured past.

honesty_audit.risk: The cap optimizer and aging projection are skaters-only; the team cap summary excludes goalies (not in shift data).
honesty_audit.mitigation: Documented as future work; goalie market value exists separately with its own (volatile, small-sample) rate.

honesty_audit.risk: Goalie WAR is high-variance and small-sample; a single hot/cold season swings a goalie's surplus a lot.
honesty_audit.mitigation: Flagged on every goalie row; surplus read as a wide range.

meta.northStar: Every metric ships with its own calibration / reliability report.
